parsed_review.summary:
CP00-344 is a Risk-A, 10-unit descriptor-only closeout pack covering RP11.P02.M07.S11-S20 (the "Test And Golden Case Set" service tail: lock acquisition rule, persistence boundary, validation error mapping, review/approval-required routing, blocked-claim output, rollback/retry behavior, and happy/denied unit tests). The pack-level claim is supported and accurate.

Verified consistency across plan, manifest, registry, contract, and validator: docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (line 413) pins CP00-344 = Risk A / 10 units / range RP11.P02.M07.S11-RP11.P02.M07.S20; the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, registry TIME_EXPENSE_CORE_CP344_PACK_BINDING, contract current_pack + service_slice_descriptor + historical_pack_bindings (ends CP00-344), and the validator's deepEqual pins all agree. Deliverable distribution (ui:2, implementation:3, claude_review:1, failure_recovery:2, test:2) and micro_title distribution ({"Test And Golden Case Set":10}) match the manifest's included_units exactly. Row keys for all 10 titles are unique and each receives its expected extras from the inherited CP343 row-extras map.

Descriptor-only boundary is enforced in both producer and checker: service.js only builds frozen descriptor objects (no fs writes, no network, no DB, no real-data load), every M07 row carries descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false / customer_safe_errors_only=true / raw_payload_included=false plus the shared service guards (acquires_runtime_lock=false, creates/updates_database_rows=false, performs_rollback_runtime=false, performs_retry_runtime=false, executes_unit_test_runtime_paths=false, validation/blocked-claim/lock-token detail excluded, unit_test_denied_path.expected_outcome="denied_customer_safe"); validators.js validateTimeExpenseCoreCp344* re-checks each of these and the coverage/distribution invariants. The manifest implementation_summary's long no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data attestation list is consistent with the code.

production_ready gate is honestly held at false (runtime_ready=false); production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification marker only, with runtime opening deferred to CP00-345 — the gate is not weakened. Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claude_packet.read_only=true, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation. Hermes appears only as deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false) with no product-runtime execution.

Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied for this run: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, well-formed JSON), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, JSON output format — no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, or tool-call-shaped output. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this pre-review stage and are generated downstream per the documented gated sequence; their absence is expected, not a defect. Unrelated dirty/untracked files (the .docx, workbook/* docs, ldip JSON) are correctly partitioned as out-of-scope and were not treated as part of this pack. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
